text.title: go-goroutines Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-goroutines as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Capturing loop variables in goroutines without passing them as parameters.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The loop variable changes; all goroutines may see the final value. Always pass as a parameter.

failure-mode.description: Goroutine leaks: spawning goroutines that never exit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Goroutines are cheap but not free. Leaks accumulate and exhaust memory. Always provide an exit condition.

failure-mode.description: Data races: concurrent access to shared mutable state without synchronization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Go detects races with go test -race. Fix them immediately; they cause unpredictable behavior in production.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: While a goroutine holds a mutex, no other goroutine can acquire it. If you block (wait for I/O, channel receive), other goroutines starve.

failure-mode.description: Sharing mutable state without channels or locks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leads to data races. Always synchronize access to shared data.
